title: BlackSky (BKSY) Q1 2026 Earnings Summary
text: Key Financial Results: BlackSky reported Q1 2026 revenue of $20.8 million, with space-based intelligence services climbing 14% sequentially. The company raised its full-year revenue guidance to $130-$150 million (previously $120-$145 million), representing over 30% growth at the midpoint. Adjusted EBITDA guidance improved to $12-$24 million annually, with a 13% margin at midpoint. The company ended the quarter with $117.5 million in cash and equivalents, plus total liquidity exceeding $195 million. Business Momentum: Management highlighted an inflection point driven by Gen-3 satellite performance. The company secured approximately $160 million in year-to-date contract awards, including a $99 million U.S. Air Force Research Lab contract for optical payload development. CEO Brian O'Toole stated: Gen-3 continues to exceed expectations, delivering exceptional 35-centimeter imaging performance. Four Gen-3 satellites are now operational, with the company targeting at least eight on-orbit by year-end. Subscription Growth: The subscription-based space intelligence business is expected to grow over 50% in 2026, reaching an annual run rate exceeding $100 million with approximately 80% gross margins. The company reported a backlog of $351 million as of quarter-end, expanding to roughly $380 million including post-quarter contracts, with $90 million projected for 2026 revenue recognition. Operational Highlights: Customers are receiving imagery with AI analytics processing in under 40 minutes. The company reported detecting and classifying over 5 million objects within days of customer operations. Management emphasized that continued growth doesn't depend on additional satellite launches this year.
